creator: vagelid814@3mkz.com
text:
Despite the fact that I've just attempted this with www/qutebrowser (which utilizes www/qt5-webengine), not www/chromium, this could in any case be applicable (practically a similar code, all things considered):

The accompanying two fixes basically #ifdef out video catch code:
 - fix src_3rdparty_chromium_media_capture_video_linux_video__capture__device__factory__linux.cc
 - fix src_3rdparty_chromium_media_capture_video_linux_video__capture__device__linux.cc

With those patches, VideoCaptureDeviceFactoryLinux::HasUsableFormats generally returns misleading, so proficient gadgets are not even identified. I've eliminated those #ifdefs in www/qt5-webengine, and I've been utilizing Facebook Courier and Jitsi from qutebrowser for a couple of days now on a HardenedBSD 13.0-CURRENT PC.

All things considered, I couldn't say whether those patches fix some issue that just fortunately don't happen on my case, or they were simply left there coincidentally. I've attempted git - - follow and web look, yet couldn't find the explanation they exist.  URL: https://svnweb.freebsd.org/changeset/ports/531050 and also you can check out https://worldapparelent.com/
